methods.description: This method applies a 6-parametric spectral expansion formula (Eq. 4), derived from the rovibrational Schrödinger equation with a Morse potential, to experimental rovibrational data of diatomic molecules. A least squares fitting routine is used to determine the best values of the spectral parameters {ω, ωx, α, B, C, D}. The fitted parameters are then used to predict rovibrational spectra and calculate potential parameters {r0, D0, α} using specific relations. The fitting procedure uses statistical weights taken as equal to one, and uncertainties are reported as one standard deviation.

steps.description: Select diatomic molecules for analysis, such as HF, 132 XeH+, and 7 LiH.
steps.searchable_summary: Select target diatomic molecules for rovibrational analysis.

steps.description: Obtain experimental rovibrational energy levels or transition frequencies for the selected molecules.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch experimental rovibrational data for selected molecules.
outputs: Experimental rovibrational data (energy levels or frequencies) with uncertainties

steps.description: Determine the best values of the spectral parameters {ω, ωx, α, B, C, D} by applying a least squares routine to the experimental data using the full 6-parametric spectral expansion formula (Eq. 4). Statistical weights are taken as equal to one.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform 6-parametric least squares fitting of experimental rovibrational data to the spectral expansion formula.
outputs: Fitted spectral parameters {ω, ωx, α, B, C, D}
Sigma standard error of the fit

steps.description: Optionally, determine the spectral parameters using the 5-parametric version of the formula (Eq. 4 with C = 0) via a least squares routine.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform 5-parametric least squares fitting of experimental rovibrational data.
outputs: Fitted spectral parameters {ω, ωx, α, B, D}
Sigma standard error of the fit

steps.description: Calculate rovibrational transition frequencies using the fitted spectral parameters obtained from both the 5-parametric and 6-parametric fits.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate rovibrational transition frequencies from fitted spectral parameters.
inputs: Fitted spectral parameters (from Step 3 and Step 4)

steps.description: Calculate the potential parameters {r0, D0, α} from the fitted spectral parameters using specific relations (Eqs. 7-9 and others). The set of spectral parameters used for this calculation is chosen based on having the relatively smallest standard errors (e.g., {ω,ωx, B} for HF and 132 XeH+, {ω, α, B} for 7 LiH).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate molecular potential parameters from selected fitted spectral parameters.
inputs: Specific relations for potential parameters (Eqs. 7-9 etc.)
Criteria for selecting spectral parameters (e.g., smallest standard errors)

steps.description: Present the calculated frequencies, the sigma standard errors of the fit, the spectral parameters obtained by the fitting procedure, and the potential parameters calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: Report and present results of rovibrational analysis.
inputs: Sigma standard errors of fit (from Step 3 and 4)
